patches.summary: Replace static "year-2026" --window default with a defaultYearWindow() helper that returns the current calendar year.
patches.reason: A hardcoded year silently produces empty result sets after calendar rollover. The helper evaluates at flag-declaration time so defaults stay correct without a rebuild.

patches.summary: Stop AuthHeader() from unconditionally re-stamping AuthSource on every call; only set it when empty.
patches.reason: Load() already classifies AuthSource as "env:AMAZON_COOKIES" or "config". Re-stamping caused config-file cookies to be reported as env-var cookies in auth status.

patches.summary: Swap fmt.Sprintf + sqlite3 shell-out for database/sql + bind parameter in countCookiesForDomain.
patches.reason: The query interpolated host_key via Sprintf; while the current call site passes a hardcoded pattern, parameterizing closes the SQL-injection class for future callers. Added auth_count_cookies_test.go with UNION ALL and trailing-OR injection cases.

patches.summary: maskToken returns "[redacted N chars]" instead of "...<last 4 chars>".
patches.reason: For long semicolon-joined Cookie headers the trailing 4 chars verbatim leak the tail of the final cookie value. Length-only output keeps the "have a token / don't" signal without exposing raw bytes.

Follow-up to greptile-countcookies-sql-injection: countCookiesForDomain no longer shells out to sqlite3, so the pre-flight guard is spurious and silently broke profile auto-detection on hosts without sqlite3 in PATH.

patches.summary: Tighten cache, store, and config directory/file modes to owner-only (0o700 / 0o600).
patches.reason: writeCache used 0o644/0o755, so cached Amazon response bodies (full order history, items, addresses, payments) were world-readable on shared hosts (CI runners, multi-user dev boxes, Docker images with multiple UIDs). Same defect existed in store.OpenWithContext and config.save().

patches.summary: auth export delegates cookie selection to cfg.AuthHeader() instead of preferring AccessToken over AmazonCookies.
patches.reason: AuthHeader() prefers AmazonCookies (env var) first, but auth export read AccessToken first. Under the documented AMAZON_COOKIES headless-agent flow, export would have dumped a stale persisted token instead of the active session bytes.

patches.summary: ClearTokens() zeroes every toml:-tagged auth-bearing field (AmazonCookies, AuthHeaderVal, AccessToken) before save().
patches.reason: AuthHeader() checks AuthHeaderVal, AmazonCookies, and AccessToken in priority order; any one left non-empty keeps the CLI authenticated, and toml.Marshal re-serializes every toml:-tagged field. Without clearing all three, `auth logout` while AMAZON_COOKIES is set (or an auth_header is configured on disk) silently persists the live session to ~/.config/amazon-orders-pp-cli/config.toml.

patches.summary: lateOrders compares ETA against today's midnight, not the current instant.
patches.reason: time.Parse("2006-01-02", ...) returns midnight UTC; eta.Before(now) marked any ETA=today order as late once UTC time passed 00:00, producing false positives throughout the delivery day. Comparing eta.Before(today-at-midnight) only flags orders whose ETA date has fully passed.

patches.summary: arrivingByDay pre-filters through inflightOrders() so cancelled-after-shipping orders with a future ETA do not surface.
patches.reason: lateOrders and where-is-my-stuff already restrict their input to inflightOrders(orders). arrivingByDay iterated the raw slice, so an order Amazon marked Cancelled after the carrier handoff (real Amazon scenario — the listing HTML keeps the future ETA but flips the status) would still appear in `arriving-soon`. Consistent inflight gating across the novel commands keeps the contract uniform.

patches.summary: Store.ListIDs double-quotes the resourceType identifier with embedded-quote escaping.
patches.reason: fmt.Sprintf("SELECT id FROM %s", resourceType) inlined the table name unquoted. Today's callers pass hardcoded values, but parameter placeholders don't work for SQL identifiers — double-quoting plus escaping embedded quotes is the SQLite-safe pattern and closes the injection class for future callers that might forward a --resources flag.

patches.summary: auth export records cfg.AuthSource (env:AMAZON_COOKIES, config, browser) instead of a hardcoded "chrome".
patches.reason: After greptile-auth-export-uses-authheader delegated cookie selection to AuthHeader(), the exported PortableSession.Source still claimed "chrome" even when the live cookies came from AMAZON_COOKIES, an `auth import --stdin`, or the TOML file — misleading 1Password and headless-agent inspection. Load() already classifies the source authoritatively, so we forward that and only fall back to "chrome" if it is unset.

patches.summary: Pass cleanDomain and cookiePath as sys.argv to the pycookiecheat helper instead of fmt.Sprintf-interpolating them into the Python script string.
patches.reason: A Chrome profile directory whose name contains a double-quote character could break out of the inline string literal and execute arbitrary Python on Linux/macOS (extractViaPycookiecheat). Although Chrome itself never creates such names, discoverChromeProfiles will pick up any directory with a Preferences + Cookies layout. Switching to sys.argv removes the interpolation entirely and closes the injection class.

patches.summary: rollupSpend propagates OrderSummary.Currency to SpendBucket.Currency instead of hardcoding "USD"; falls back to "USD" only when the parser couldn't extract one.
patches.reason: parser.parseOrderCard already extracts the per-order currency (e.g. "GBP" on amazon.co.uk, "EUR" on amazon.de). Hardcoding "USD" in the rollup mislabeled every non-US user's spend buckets. Freezing on the first order's currency per bucket is safe because a single Amazon TLD only emits one currency. Added spend_test.go covering both the GBP/EUR propagation and the empty-currency USD fallback.
